Backlog.title: Blog: Agent-Based Order Flow Simulation
Backlog.notes: Write a technical blog post about the tick engine. Cover the 4 agent types (institutional, retail, momentum, mean-reversion), Hawkes process clustering for realistic tick arrival, simulated limit order book, and how this creates authentic microstructure. Tone: engineering team. ~800-1000 words.

Backlog.title: Blog: Why Random Walks Dont Look Like Markets
Backlog.notes: Write a blog post explaining why naive Brownian motion fails as market simulation. Cover: no regime structure, unrealistic wick ratios, no volume correlation, no session structure. Then explain what PatternFoundry does differently (regime switching, session dampening, microstructure). Tone: accessible but technical. ~800 words.

Backlog.title: Blog: Building a Simulated Limit Order Book
Backlog.notes: Write about how the DOM/order book simulation works. Cover: why DOM matters for futures traders, how we simulate bids/asks, liquidity pool placement, how the book responds to aggressive orders, and what traders can practice with it. Tone: engineering team. ~700 words.

Backlog.title: Blog: The Problem with Backtesting on Historical Data
Backlog.notes: Write about limitations of replaying historical data for practice (memorization, hindsight bias, limited scenarios, curve fitting). Then present the case for generated data with known statistical properties as a complement. Position PatternFoundry as deliberate practice tool, not a backtester replacement. Tone: opinionated but fair. ~800 words.

Backlog.title: Write SEO page: "free futures trading simulator"
Backlog.notes: Target keyword: "free futures trading simulator". Compare to Forex Tester ($300), NinjaTrader (complex). Highlight: browser-based, instant, no download.

Backlog.notes: 5-8 min walkthrough. Only do this if HN/X generate interest. No pressure.

Ongoing.title: Ship features > market features
Ongoing.notes: Best marketing for a technical product: keep making it better. Every feature shipped is a new post and a new reason for word-of-mouth.
